graded_findings.rationale: The gather item reports this document was updated appropriately. There is no adherence gap to grade as merge-relevant.
The gather item reports this reference removed the obsolete environment variable. This is not a substantive documentation defect.
The gather item reports this dscli documentation was reconciled with the new sample-rate flags. No blocking or advisory issue is present for this ref.
The gather item reports the index now routes logging sampler questions to the new design document. This is not a remaining documentation gap.
The gather item reports the logging overview now covers LogSampler responsibilities and links to the design. There is no substantive issue to block on.
The gather item reports the new design document covers the relevant sampler architecture and operational constraints. This positive finding is not an adherence gap.

This shared logging design still documents the removed log_rate_limit and DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT mechanism. Leaving stale source-backed design context for a removed config path is a real adherence gap.
This trace context doc still describes request sampling as derived from local log_rate_limit. That conflicts with the new request_sample_rate and LogSampler flow in reusable context.
This design plan still frames slow logs around bypassing log_rate_limit sampling. The stale semantics can mislead future work on diagnostic and request sampler behavior.

This implementation summary still references log_rate_limit request sampling after the sampler behavior changed. That is a substantive repository-context mismatch that should be corrected before merge.
